title: Bridge Essay: White matter pathways and social cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**White matter pathways and social cognition.**. Neurosci Biobehav Rev.. PMID: 29684403. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2018.04.015. There is agrowing consensus that social cognition and behavior emerge from interactions across distributed regions of the "social brain". Researchers have traditionally focused their attention on functional response properties of these gray matter networks and neglected the vital role of white matter connections in establishing such networks and their functions.
